headline: Move better.
Get stronger.
Get back on your feet.
hero.lead: 22 Strong Foundation helps veterans, low income youth, and others access hands-on recovery care when insurance, cost, or other barriers get in the way.

mission.heading: Specialized recovery care for people whose next appointment is at risk.
mission.lead: A veteran or young athlete can have a real injury, a referral, and a care plan, and still lose progress when the next appointment is not covered, delayed, or out of reach. That is the gap 22 Strong exists to close.
needParagraphs: Typical referrals arrive at the same pressure point: progress has started, but cost, coverage limits, timing, or the lack of the right connection puts the next visit at risk.

So far in 2026, 22 Strong has funded over 200 recovery sessions for 45 people, and referrals continue to increase as providers, veterans, coaches, and community partners identify more people whose recovery is stalled by cost or coverage.

With an average care plan of about $850, current donor funding cannot meet every request. 22 Strong receives referrals, verifies the access gap, and pays for recovery sessions directly so care can continue before progress is lost.

story.heading: The gap kept showing up in the treatment room.
paragraphs: 22 Strong began inside Next Level Wellness, a sports-medicine center in Newport Beach co-owned by Travis Mayfield and Dr. Vasili “Dr. Vas” Gatsinaris. In the treatment room, they saw the same problem repeat: the care was working, but access to the next visit was not guaranteed.

Travis, a Purple Heart veteran of the 101st Airborne in Vietnam, knew what a long recovery can demand. Dr. Vas had built a hands-on practice around restoring mobility and function. Together, they saw that high-level recovery care should not depend on whether someone can afford one more appointment.

They built 22 Strong to turn that lesson into a funding path: veterans serving veterans, providers and community partners making referrals, and donor dollars paying for care when coverage or cash would stop the plan.

audiences.heading: Veterans
audiences.lead: Care funding for veterans rebuilding strength, mobility, and function after service.
audiences.body: Support that respects the long work of recovery and helps keep care moving when benefits, timing, or access are not enough.

audiences.heading: Low income youth
audiences.lead: Recovery support for young athletes whose families can't cover the care it takes to get back after an injury.
audiences.body: Support for families facing a season-ending injury, limited coverage, or care that stops before recovery is done.

luke.story: Luke shares why access to hands-on recovery care matters after service.

adam.quote: A major part of my success was the treatments and therapy I received from Dr. Vas.
adam.story: During my sophomore year in college, I had hip surgery that was potentially season-ending and even career-ending. My coaches referred me to Dr. Vas, who was able to take care of me and eventually get me back to playing within a short few weeks. That season I ended up winning hustle player of the year award. Throughout my career, I saw Dr. Vas on various occasions for injuries that came up, along with maintenance and preventive care. I was able to play competitively at the top level.

veteranVoices.name: Tom Boscamp
veteranVoices.title: U.S. Army Veteran · Veteran Services Coordinator · OCVMFC Steering Committee · NAVPA Board Member
veteranVoices.quote: I am a 65 year old Veteran who needs to be active since I am on the go taking care of Veterans in the Community. I have degenerative disk disease and Spinal Stenosis as well as radiculopathy. The VA treatments are far and few between as they do not have enough chiropractors, massage therapists, and Physical therapy specialists. Thankfully Dr. Vas has filled that void and with his treatments it has allowed me to continue being active. I have even lost 15 pounds! I can’t say enough how good Next Level Wellness is! Also their commitment to serving Veterans is “Next Level”!

veteranVoices.name: CSM (Ret.) Ramon Rodriguez
veteranVoices.title: U.S. Army Special Forces · Vietnam War · 3 Silver Stars · 5 Purple Hearts · Ranger Hall of Fame
veteranVoices.quote: Years of pain had limited what I could do. After treatment here, I got back mobility I thought was gone for good.

veteranVoices.name: SGT Mark Miller
veteranVoices.title: U.S. Army · 101st Airborne Division · Vietnam War · Bronze Star with “V” Device
veteranVoices.quote: I came in skeptical, but the therapy worked. I have less pain now and I'm back to handling things on my own.

veteranVoices.name: John Baca
veteranVoices.title: U.S. Army · Vietnam War · Medal of Honor Recipient
veteranVoices.quote: I could barely get around when I started. A few weeks in, I'm walking better and getting through a normal day again.

strongCircle.heading: Monthly support that keeps care moving.
strongCircle.lead: The Strong Circle is the monthly giving community that funds care before a recovery gap becomes a stopped plan.
strongCircle.subhead: A steady $22 helps us say yes faster
strongCircle.cardTitle: What a monthly gift makes possible.
strongCircle.cardBody: Recurring gifts help 22 Strong plan ahead for people referred by providers, coaches, veterans, and community partners.

modal.heading: Join the Strong Circle.
modal.lead: A steady monthly gift gives 22 Strong a reliable base for appointments and follow-through — not just the first visit.

proof.label: Steady support
proof.value: Funds care beyond the first visit.

heading: A recovery gap appears.
22 Strong helps close it.
howItWorks.kicker: The story is not complicated: someone has a real recovery plan, the next appointment becomes uncertain, and donor funding keeps the care moving.

steps.heading: Referral or request
steps.body: Referrals come from the VA, veteran networks, coaches, school counselors, and local community organizations.

steps.heading: We evaluate the gap
steps.body: 22 Strong reviews the situation. If coverage has run out, private care is out of reach, or the right connections aren’t there — we step in.

steps.heading: Care is funded
steps.body: Gifts cover recovery sessions directly, at no cost to the person receiving care.

funding.heading: Appointments, not abstractions.
funding.body: Funding supports practical recovery care for people with a clear need and a real access gap.

items.heading: Hands-on recovery sessions
items.body: Chiropractic, physical therapy, and massage therapy.

items.heading: Follow-through after the first visit
items.body: Help staying with care long enough for recovery to keep moving.

items.heading: Provider and partner referrals
items.body: A path for veterans, families, and neighbors who are already trying to get well.

howItWorks.note: Care is currently provided through Next Level Fitness & Wellness Center in Newport Beach — high-level sports medicine, including Fascial Integrative Treatment (FIT). It treats injuries like rotator cuff, knee, neck, lower back, hip, ankle/foot, and wrist/elbow pain. As donor support grows, 22 Strong can expand toward a network of vetted providers across Southern California.

leadership.name: Dr. Vasili “Dr. Vas” Gatsinaris, D.C.
paragraphs: Dr. Vas earned his Doctorate of Chiropractic from the Los Angeles College of Chiropractic in 2001 and has more than 25 years of experience helping people recover and rebuild.

Over his career, he has worked with everyone from professional athletes to first responders, developing a hands-on, integrative approach focused on restoring mobility and getting people back to life stronger and faster.
He is co-owner of Next Level Fitness & Wellness Center in Newport Beach — the same recovery care 22 Strong helps fund.

leadership.name: Travis Mayfield
paragraphs: A Purple Heart recipient who served as a Sergeant with the U.S. Army’s 101st Airborne Division in Vietnam, Travis is Co-Owner and President of Next Level Fitness & Wellness Center. His own long road back from war is the inspiration behind 22 Strong’s model. He helped found the foundation and leads its mission to make recovery care accessible to those who served and to others rebuilding their lives.
His service and community work have been recognized by the California State Senate and Assembly, the U.S. House of Representatives, and Orange County.

donate.heading: Fund the next recovery appointment.
donate.lead: Every gift helps turn a stalled recovery plan into appointments someone can actually reach.
donate.outcomes: In 2026, 22 Strong has funded over 200 recovery sessions for 45 people across Orange County.
donate.goalCopy: $22,000 funds around 100 recovery sessions — keeping care within reach for the people who need it most.

givingInvite.heading: Keep care moving every month.
givingInvite.body: Join the Strong Circle for $22/month and help 22 Strong say yes before a recovery plan stops.

contact.heading: Start the right conversation with 22 Strong.
